title: Bounded string processing deterministic evidence bank

items.title: Bounded string processing: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? strncpy always creates a null-terminated string and therefore makes arbitrary source text safe.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Do not assume strncpy terminates; design from destination capacity, validate source bounds, and establish the terminator explicitly when needed.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Do not assume strncpy terminates; design from destination capacity, validate source bounds, and establish the terminator explicitly when needed.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Do not assume strncpy terminates; design from destination capacity, validate source bounds, and establish the terminator explicitly when needed.
strncpy always creates a null-terminated string and therefore makes arbitrary source text safe.

answer.explanation: Do not assume strncpy terminates; design from destination capacity, validate source bounds, and establish the terminator explicitly when needed.

items.title: Bounded string processing: executable application
items.prompt: Read at most 40 lowercase ASCII letters, reject other characters with invalid, and print the string reversed without writing outside the array.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: capacity-aware bounded string transformation

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises capacity-aware bounded string transformation under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
